title: 15 Ladakh Tour Packages From Kolkata | Experience The Best Of Ladakh
description: Book from 15+ curated Ladakh tour packages from Kolkata. Enjoy seamless flights, stays, and guided tours to Pangong Lake, Nubra Valley & Leh.

collectiondata.overview:
Best Tour Packages for Ladakh from India – Explore the Land of High Passes

Looking for the best tour packages for Ladakh from India? Experience the breathtaking beauty and unique culture of Ladakh, known as the Land of High Passes. Whether you’re a solo traveler, a couple on honeymoon packages, or an adventure enthusiast, our carefully curated Ladakh Tour Packages offer unforgettable journeys through some of India’s most spectacular landscapes.

(For official updates on permits and travel advisories, always check the Ladakh Tourism website or government portals before planning your trip.)

Leh Ladakh Tour Packages

Why Choose Ladakh for Your Next Adventure?

Stunning Landscapes and Unique Culture
Ladakh captivates travelers with its stark mountain vistas, serene lakes, and ancient monasteries. The blend of Tibetan Buddhist culture and remote Himalayan beauty makes Ladakh a one-of-a-kind destination for all types of travelers. Consider engaging local guides to enrich your cultural experience and support the community.

Adventure Activities and Spiritual Experiences
From thrilling bike tours on rugged terrain to peaceful visits to monasteries, Ladakh offers diverse experiences. Enjoy trekking, river rafting, and camel safaris, or attend traditional festivals to immerse yourself in local customs. Note that adventure activities require appropriate fitness levels and sometimes permits.
Ideal for Solo Travelers, Couples, and Adventure Enthusiasts
Our best value Ladakh tour packages for solo travelers are designed to provide safety, community, and exploration. Couples can choose from budget-friendly Ladakh honeymoon packages featuring romantic stays and scenic spots, while adventure seekers can pick adrenaline-pumping bike tours. We recommend booking well in advance during peak seasons (June–September) due to high demand.

Top Ladakh Tour Packages from India

7 Days Ladakh Tour Package from India – Classic Itinerary
This itinerary covers all major highlights, including Leh, Pangong Lake, Nubra Valley, and Tso Moriri. It’s perfect for those seeking a comprehensive experience with comfortable accommodation and guided tours. Be sure to check the specific inclusion of acclimatization days to prevent altitude sickness.

Ladakh Bike Tour Packages from India – For Thrill Seekers
Explore Ladakh on two wheels with our specially curated bike tours. These packages combine adventure with breathtaking scenery, suitable for experienced riders and those wanting to test their limits. Verify whether your package includes safety gear, bike rental insurance, and emergency support.

Ladakh Family Tour Packages from India
Our Ladakh tour packages from Delhi also include options tailored for families, offering child-friendly activities and easy-paced sightseeing to accommodate all ages. Always clarify accommodation options and vehicle types to ensure comfort for seniors and children.

Customized Ladakh Tour Packages for Indian Travelers
Looking for something specific? We offer personalized Leh Ladakh tour packages from Delhi and Kolkata to match your interests, budget, and time. Custom packages can help you avoid crowded spots and focus on your preferred experiences.

Must-Visit Places in Ladakh Tour Packages

Leh – The Heart of Ladakh
The vibrant town of Leh serves as the base for most tours. Visit the Leh Palace, Shanti Stupa, and bustling local markets for handicrafts and traditional food. Try local Ladakhi cuisine but be cautious if you have dietary restrictions.

Pangong Lake – Famous for Its Stunning Blue Waters
Pangong Lake is a must-see for its crystal-clear, ever-changing blue hues. It’s included in nearly all Ladakh tour packages from Delhi and is a highlight for couples and solo travelers alike. Due to environmental regulations, camping near the lake is limited; verify your package’s lodging options.

Nubra Valley – Sand Dunes and Monasteries
Experience the unique desert-like landscapes, double-humped camels, and ancient monasteries in Nubra Valley, an integral part of many Ladakh tour package from Kolkata itineraries. Remember to carry warm clothes as temperatures drop sharply at night.

Tso Moriri Lake – Serene and Less Crowded
For travelers seeking tranquility away from the crowds, Tso Moriri offers pristine beauty and a chance to witness local wildlife. This is a remote area; confirm medical access and emergency plans with your operator.

Magnetic Hill and Khardung La Pass
Don’t miss the world-famous Magnetic Hill, where vehicles appear to defy gravity, and Khardung La Pass, one of the highest motorable roads in the world. Check weather and road conditions in advance, especially outside peak season.

What’s Included in the Best Ladakh Tour Packages from India?

Comfortable Accommodation and Meals
Our best tour packages for Ladakh from India include stays at comfortable guesthouses, hotels, and camps, often with local cuisine provided. Confirm if meals accommodate dietary preferences or allergies.

Airport Transfers and Local Transportation
Travel hassle-free with included airport pickups and private transport for all sightseeing and excursions. Verify vehicle types to ensure suitability for your group size and comfort preferences.

Guided Sightseeing and Adventure Activities
Experienced guides accompany you on cultural tours, adventure activities, and offbeat experiences tailored to your package. Ask about guide certifications and language proficiency before booking.

Best Time to Visit Ladakh from India

Summer Season – May to September
The most popular time for travel, with pleasant weather ideal for sightseeing, trekking, and outdoor activities. Advance booking is highly recommended during this season.

Winter Season – For Snow Adventures
Experience Ladakh’s snowy magic with limited-access winter tours focused on snow sports and cultural immersion. Be prepared for extreme cold and limited services.

Tips for Traveling in High Altitude Regions

Acclimatize gradually, stay hydrated, and consult your doctor if you have health concerns. We include acclimatization days in all best tour packages for Ladakh from India. Avoid alcohol and heavy exercise during early days.

Frequently Asked Questions About Ladakh Tour Packages from India

Do I need any special permits for Ladakh tours?
Yes, certain areas require Inner Line Permits, which are typically arranged as part of your Ladakh tour packages from Delhi or Kolkata. Permit fees and procedures can change; always verify with official sources before travel.

What is the best duration for a Ladakh trip?
A 7 to 10-day itinerary is recommended to cover major sites without rushing and allow for proper acclimatization.

How to acclimatize to high altitude in Ladakh?
Rest during the first day in Leh, avoid alcohol, and stay hydrated. Our packages always include guidance on acclimatization.

Are there affordable Ladakh tour packages for couples?
Absolutely. Our affordable Ladakh tour packages for couples offer cozy stays, romantic views like Pangong Lake, and personalized services. Couples should book early to secure the best room options.

Do you provide budget-friendly Ladakh honeymoon packages?
Yes. Budget-friendly Ladakh honeymoon packages include highlights such as serene lakes, monasteries, and private excursions suited for couples. These options are especially popular among honeymoon packages from Kolkata.

What are the best value Ladakh tour packages for solo travelers?
Our best value Ladakh tour packages for solo travelers offer safety, group activities, and comfortable accommodation with flexible itineraries.

How to Book the Best Ladakh Tour Packages from India

Trusted Travel Agencies and Online Platforms
Book through reputed agencies that offer verified reviews and transparent pricing, ensuring your Ladakh tour package from Delhi or Kolkata is reliable and well-organized. TravelXploria is considered among the best domestic tour operators in India, known for offering exceptional value as one of the best tour and travel company in India and the best tours and travel agency in India.

Important Tips Before Booking Your Ladakh Tour
Check cancellation policies, confirm what’s included, and verify the health and fitness requirements, especially for high-altitude travel.

Essential Documents and Packing List for Ladakh

1. Valid photo ID
2. Inner Line Permits (arranged through most packages)
3. Warm clothing (even in summer)
4. Sunscreen and lip balm
5. Personal medications and altitude sickness pills
6. Comfortable trekking shoes
7. Power bank and flashlight

packages.overview:
1. Arrive Leh: Land at 3,500 m and acclimatize.

  2. Leh → Nubra Valley: Cross Khardung La, the world’s highest motorable pass.

  3. Nubra Valley → Pangong Lake: Drive along the Shayok River to the blue waters of Pangong.

  4. Pangong Lake → Leh: Return via Hemis Monastery for an overnight in Leh.

  5. Leh → Kargil: Scenic transfer along the Indus River.

  6. Kargil → Srinagar: 210 km drive through mountain passes into the Kashmir Valley.

  7. Srinagar → Gulmarg → Srinagar: Day trip to Gulmarg and back.

  8. Depart Srinagar: Transfer to airport or railway for onward journey.

Overnight stay at the hotel.

After an early breakfast, embark on a scenic drive to Nubra Valley via Khardungla Pass (18,380 ft), the world’s highest motorable road and the gateway to the Siachen Glacier. Enjoy breathtaking panoramic views from the top of the pass. Accompanied by our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria, visit Deskit Monastery (118 km), founded by Lama Sherab Zangpo in 1420 A.D. Upon arrival, check in at your pre-arranged camp or hotel. Later, visit Hundar village (7 km from Deskit) and experience the unique Double-Humped Bactrian Camel Safari in the mesmerizing sand dunes between Hundar and Deskit villages. Dinner and overnight stay at a hotel or camp in Nubra.

After breakfast at the hotel, embark on a scenic drive to Pangong Lake via Shyok, Tangtse, and Spangmik, accompanied by our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria. This route is a breathtaking shortcut offering mesmerizing landscapes. The road follows the Shyok River, ascending through rugged mountains before descending into Shayok village, a picturesque settlement surrounded by barren yet stunning terrain. Enjoy the beauty of this serene village before continuing your journey. By the afternoon, arrive at the magnificent Pangong Lake, where the crystal-clear blue waters and the majestic mountains create a surreal landscape. Spend the rest of the day soaking in the beauty of the lake. Overnight stay at a camp near Pangong Lake.

En route, visit the magnificent Thiksay Monastery, a 12-storied monastery of the Gelugpa order, perched on an island-like mountain offering spectacular views. The monastery is renowned for its grandeur and spiritual significance. Continue the journey with a visit to Sindhu Ghat, a serene riverside location with deep cultural and historical importance. Upon arrival in Leh, enjoy an evening at leisure exploring the local markets, where you can shop for souvenirs, handicrafts, and traditional Ladakhi items. Dinner and overnight stay at a hotel in Leh.

– Srinagar ( 210 Km. )","day_summary":"

After an early breakfast at 6:00 AM, embark on a scenic drive to Srinagar, accompanied by our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria. En route, pass through Drass, the second coldest inhabited place on earth, where you will witness spectacular views of the famous Kargil War Peaks – Tiger Hill and Tololing Peak. Stop at the Vijay Smarak, a war memorial dedicated to the brave Indian soldiers who sacrificed their lives during the Kargil War in 1999, and pay tribute to their valor. Continue the journey through mesmerizing landscapes and reach Srinagar by late afternoon. Upon arrival, check in at your hotel and relax. Overnight stay at the hotel in Srinagar.

In the morning, proceed for Gulmarg, also known as the \"Meadow of Flowers\", situated 2,730 meters above sea level. Accompanied by our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria, embark on a scenic 60 km drive, which takes approximately 2 hours. Gulmarg is renowned for having one of the best ski slopes in the world and the highest golf course with 18 holes. If the weather permits, marvel at the stunning view of Nanga Parbat, the \"Naked Mountain\", standing at over 26,000 feet, dominating the entire region. Experience an exhilarating cable car ride (Gondola) to Khilangmarg, offering breathtaking panoramic views. In the evening, drive back to Srinagar and unwind at your accommodation. Dinner and overnight stay at the hotel or a deluxe houseboat in Srinagar.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Depart srinagar","day_summary":"

The tour concludes as our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria ensures a seamless and punctual transfer to the domestic airport. We at Travelxploria sincerely thank you for your patronage and look forward to welcoming you on another unforgettable journey in the future. Safe travels.

packages.overview:
1. Arrive Leh
    Land at Leh airport (3,505 m / 11,567 ft) and transfer to your hotel.
    Spend the afternoon acclimatizing and wandering through the bustling Leh bazaar.

  2. Leh → Kargil (234 km)
    Embark on a scenic drive along the Indus River, passing rugged gorges and roadside villages.
    Enjoy vistas of snow‑capped peaks as you approach Kargil by late afternoon.

  3. Kargil
    Visit the Kargil War Memorial to pay homage to the heroes of the 1999 conflict.
    Return to Leh in the evening, soaking in the dramatic mountain landscapes en route.

  4. Leh → Nubra Valley via Khardung La
    Depart early to conquer Khardung La (5,359 m), the world’s highest motorable pass.
    Descend into the verdant Nubra Valley, famed for its sand dunes and Bactrian camels.

  5. Nubra Valley → Pangong Lake via Shayok
    Drive alongside the winding Shayok River, framed by towering cliffs.
    Arrive at Pangong Lake in the afternoon and watch its sapphire waters shift hues.

  6. Pangong Lake → Leh (via Hemis)
    Pause at Hemis Monastery to admire its vibrant murals and ancient artifacts.
    Continue back to Leh for an overnight stay under the starry Ladakhi sky.

  7. Depart Leh
    After breakfast, transfer to Leh airport for your onward flight.
    Carry with you memories of high passes, serene lakes, and timeless Himalayan culture.

​

Upon arrival, you will be warmly received by our Travelxploria representative, experienced tour guide, and best on-ground support staff and transferred to your hotel. Enjoy complete rest during the first half of the day to acclimatize. After a delightful lunch, if suitable, proceed to visit the famous Shanti Stupa, a stunning Japanese Pagoda built for world peace by Indian-based Japanese monks and inaugurated by His Holiness the Dalai Lama in 1985, where you can witness a mesmerizing sunset. Return to the hotel for a delicious dinner and enjoy an overnight stay in Leh.

to Kargil (234 Kms)","day_summary":"

After a delightful breakfast, embark on a scenic drive to Kargil with our Travelxploria representative, experienced tour guide, and best on-ground support staff ensuring a comfortable journey. En route, stop to visit the ancient Lamayuru Monastery, a site of immense religious importance. Historically, this monastery was so revered that even criminals seeking refuge were granted sanctuary. Perched atop a hill, its age-old bastion and unique architectural design exude grandeur. After an insightful exploration, continue your journey through breathtaking landscapes, crossing the Fotu La Pass (14,300 ft), the highest pass on this route. Upon arrival in Kargil, check in to your hotel for a relaxing overnight stay.

Valley/1744976893938-AdobeStock_362520422.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Leh to Nubra Valley via kharodng la world highest mortable pass.","day_summary":"

After an early breakfast, embark on an exhilarating drive to Nubra Valley via Khardungla Pass (18,380 ft), the world’s highest motorable road and the gateway to the Siachen Glacier. The breathtaking views from the top of the pass are truly mesmerizing. Accompanied by our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria, visit the historic Deskit Monastery (118 km), founded by Lama Sherab Zangpo in 1420 A.D. Upon arrival, check in at your camp or hotel and relax. Later, explore Hundar Village (7 km from Deskit) and enjoy a Double-Humped Bactrian Camel Safari amidst the stunning sand dunes between Hundar and Deskit villages. Conclude the day with a delightful dinner and an overnight stay at your hotel/camp in Nubra Valley.

After a delicious breakfast at the hotel, embark on a scenic drive to Pangong Lake via Shyok, Tangtse, and Spangmik, accompanied by our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria. This breathtaking route, a shorter yet stunning alternative, follows the Shyok River, winding through rugged landscapes and ascending a mountain before descending into Shyok Village. In less than an hour, reach this charming village, surrounded by barren mountains, offering a truly picturesque setting. By afternoon, arrive at the serene Pangong Lake, where you can soak in the mesmerizing beauty of its crystal-clear blue waters against the backdrop of majestic mountains. Enjoy a peaceful evening by the lake before settling in for an overnight stay at the camp.

After a delightful breakfast, embark on your journey back to Leh, accompanied by our experienced tour guide from Travelxploria. En route, visit the magnificent Thiksey Monastery, a 12-storied monastery of the Gelugpa order, perched on an island mountain in a commanding position. This architectural marvel offers breathtaking views and deep spiritual significance. Continue your drive with a stop at Sindhu Ghat, a serene riverside destination known for its tranquil ambiance and cultural importance. Upon arrival in Leh, enjoy a leisurely evening exploring the local markets, where you can shop for souvenirs and experience the vibrant Ladakhi culture. End your day with a delicious dinner and an overnight stay at your hotel in Leh.

packages.package_name: The Luminous Beauty of Leh Ladakh
packages.overview:
1. Arrival & Acclimatization
    Land in Leh (3,505 m) and transfer to your hotel to rest and acclimatize.
    In the evening, visit Shanti Stupa for sweeping sunset views over the Leh Valley.

  2. Leh → Magnetic Hill → Sangam → Kargil (235 km)
    Depart early, stopping at the Magnetic Hill optical illusion and the confluence at Sangam Point.
    Continue to Kargil, arriving by late afternoon to check in and explore the town.

  3. Kargil → Leh (235 km)
    Retrace your route along the Indus River, enjoying the changing landscapes.
    Arrive back in Leh by evening and unwind at your hotel.

  4. Leh → Nubra Valley via Khardung La (135 km)
    Set off before dawn to conquer Khardung La (5,600 m), the world’s highest motorable pass.
    Descend to Diskit (3,048 m), visit Diskit Monastery, and explore Hunder’s sand dunes.

  5. Diskit/Hunder → Pangong Lake via Shayok
    Drive through the rugged Shayok River valley, lined with dramatic cliffs.
    Reach Pangong Lake by afternoon and marvel as its waters shift from blue to green.

  6. Pangong Lake → Leh
    Depart Pangong after sunrise and head back toward Leh, with a stop at Hemis Monastery if time allows.
    Arrive in Leh by evening for your final overnight stay.

  7. Departure from Leh
    After breakfast, transfer to Leh airport for your onward flight.
    Take with you memories of high passes, turquoise lakes, and timeless mountain culture.

Experience a breathtaking flight over the Himalayas, offering stunning aerial views of the majestic peaks. Upon arrival, you will be warmly received by our Travelxploria representative, along with our experienced tour guide and best on-ground support staff, and transferred to your hotel. Enjoy a refreshing welcome tea or coffee and take the entire day to rest and acclimatize to the high altitude. In the late afternoon, if health permits, embark on a scenic drive to the Shanti Stupa, a serene and spiritual site offering panoramic views of Leh. After soaking in the tranquility, return to the hotel for a comfortable overnight stay.

Magnetic Hill/ Sangam Point/ Kargil / War memorial (235 kms)","day_summary":"

After a delightful breakfast at the hotel, our Travelxploria representative, along with our experienced tour guide and best on-ground support staff, will take you on an exciting sightseeing tour. Begin with a visit to the Hall of Fame, a must-visit for every patriotic Indian, built by the Indian Army in memory of the brave soldiers who sacrificed their lives in the Kargil War. Next, proceed to the Gurudwara Patthar Sahib, located 40 km from Leh, constructed in 1571 to honor Guru Nanak’s visit to the region.

Continue to the Magnetic Hill, where you will witness a fascinating optical illusion—your car, with the engine turned off, seemingly moves uphill on its own. Then, head towards Sangam, the stunning confluence of the Zanskar and Indus rivers at Nimu, where you can also enjoy an exhilarating rafting experience on the Zanskar River.

Return to Leh in the afternoon and later visit the ancient Lamayuru Monastery, one of the oldest monasteries in Ladakh, known for its spiritual ambiance and stunning architecture. Afterward, embark on a scenic drive to Kargil (2,710 meters), our midway stop to Leh. Enjoy a comfortable overnight stay at the hotel, reflecting on the day's breathtaking experiences.

/ Leh (235 kms)","day_summary":"

Embark on a full-day scenic drive, transitioning from the lush green valleys to the barren yet mesmerizing landscapes of Ladakh. Accompanied by our Travelxploria representative, experienced tour guide, and best on-ground support staff, the journey unfolds as we ascend multiple high-altitude passes and arrive at Mulbekh, the first Buddhist village of the region. Here, witness the awe-inspiring giant statue of Maitreya Buddha, believed to have naturally existed.

Continuing our drive, we cross Fotula Pass (4,166 meters), the highest point on this route, before reaching the ancient village of Lamayuru. Stop for a delightful lunch and explore Lamayuru Monastery, the oldest monastery in Ladakh, renowned for its spiritual significance and breathtaking location.

As we descend through the lower valleys of Ladakh (Shyam region), we pass through picturesque villages like Basgo and Nimu, where we witness the spectacular confluence of the Zanskar and Indus rivers. Finally, we arrive in Leh (3,500 meters), where you will check in to your hotel and enjoy a comfortable overnight stay, reminiscing about the stunning landscapes and cultural wonders encountered along the way.

Valley/1744976893938-AdobeStock_362520422.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Nubra Valley (Diskit/Hunder) (10000 feet) via Khardung La (18380 feet) 135km.","day_summary":"

After an early breakfast, embark on an unforgettable journey to the breathtaking Nubra Valley, accompanied by our Travelxploria representative, experienced tour guide, and best on-ground support staff. Drive through the world's highest motorable road, Khardung La (18,380 ft), enjoying the zigzag trail that leads to the stunning Hundar village. Upon arrival, check in at your hotel or camp and take some time to relax.

In the afternoon, experience the mesmerizing sand dunes of Hundar, where you can enjoy a thrilling double-hump camel ride, a unique attraction of the region. Later, unwind and soak in the beauty of the landscape before heading back to your accommodation for an overnight stay at the camp or hotel.

  • Khardung La Pass: The gateway to Nubra Valley, Khardung La stands at 18,390 ft and is around 39 km from Leh. The route offers stunning views of snow-clad mountains and has checkpoints on both sides before leading into the valley.
  • Deskit Village: The administrative headquarters of Nubra Valley, Deskit is a charming village featuring a small market, a 500-year-old monastery, and spectacular panoramic views from the hilltop monastery.
  • Deskit Gompa: Established in 1420 AD by Lama Sherab Zangpo, this monastery houses around 100 monks and is a branch of the Thikse Monastery. The Gustor Festival, celebrated on the 20th and 21st day of the 12th Tibetan calendar month, is a must-see event for visitors.
  • Hundar Village: A scenic village surrounded by lush trees, Hundar offers a peaceful retreat. It is home to a small monastery and an ancient ruined fort. The camel safari (on your own) from Hundar to Deskit, through the golden sand dunes, is an unforgettable experience, lasting around two hours.

After an exciting day exploring Nubra Valley, return to your camp or hotel for a comfortable overnight stay under the starlit Ladakh sky.

/ Hunder/ Pangong Lake via Shayok","day_summary":"

Today, wake up early and embark on a mesmerizing visit to Diskit Monastery, offering a breathtaking panoramic view of Nubra Valley. Accompanied by our Travelxploria representative, experienced tour guide, and best on-ground support staff, immerse yourself in the spiritual and scenic beauty of this ancient monastery before continuing your journey.

Later, drive towards the enchanting Pangong Lake via Shyok, passing through breathtaking landscapes and rugged mountains that will leave you awe-struck. Upon arrival, witness the turquoise-colored waters of Pangong Lake, a serene oasis nestled at 14,000 feet (4,267 meters). Spend your afternoon admiring the spectacular scenery, as the ever-changing hues of blue and green reflect the majestic Changchenmo mountain range in the brackish water of the lake.

Above Spangmik, take in the view of the glacier and snow-capped peaks of the Pangong Range, creating a picture-perfect setting. After soaking in the tranquil beauty of Pangong Lake, check in at your hotel or camp for a comfortable overnight stay amidst the serenity of Ladakh.

After a leisurely breakfast, take a peaceful stroll along the serene Pangong Lake, allowing Mother Nature to soothe your senses. Accompanied by our Travelxploria representative, experienced tour guide, and best on-ground support staff, soak in the breathtaking views and capture the mesmerizing beauty of the lake.

Later, begin your scenic drive back to Leh, exploring some of Ladakh’s most iconic attractions en route. Visit the magnificent Thiksey Monastery, known for its impressive architectural grandeur and spiritual significance. Continue to Shey Palace, the former summer retreat of Ladakh’s royal family, offering stunning views of the Indus Valley.

A special stop awaits at Rancho School (3 Idiots Fame), an educational institute made famous by the Bollywood movie, before concluding the journey with a visit to the Hemis Monastery, one of Ladakh’s richest and most revered Buddhist monasteries.

Upon arrival in Leh, check in to your hotel and enjoy a relaxing overnight stay, reflecting on the unforgettable experiences of the day.

packages.overview:
1. Arrive Leh (3,505 m / 11,567 ft)
    Land at Leh airport, transfer to your hotel, and spend the afternoon acclimatizing.

  2. Drive Sham Valley & Alchi (≈70 km round‑trip)
    Cruise through the serene Sham Valley with stops at Gurudwara Pathar Sahib and Magnetic Hill, then explore the 11th‑century Alchi Monastery’s famed murals.

  3. Tour Khardung La Pass (18,380 ft) & Stok
    Depart early to conquer Khardung La, the world’s highest motorable road, then descend to Stok village to visit the royal Stok Palace Museum and stroll through mustard‑fieldled lanes.

  4. Tour Pangong Lake
    Embark on a full‑day drive via Chang La, arriving at the sapphire‑hued Pangong Lake; enjoy lakeside views as its colors shift from blue to green.

  5. Depart Leh
    After breakfast, transfer to Leh airport for your onward flight, taking with you memories of high passes and tranquil lakes.

Transfer to your hotel and enjoy a delicious breakfast before 09:30 AM. Spend half a day at rest for acclimatization to the high altitude.

After a sumptuous lunch at the hotel, embark on a guided tour to visit the iconic Shanti Stupa and Leh Palace, where our expert guide will share fascinating insights into Ladakh’s rich heritage. Later in the evening, take a leisurely stroll through the local market, soaking in the vibrant atmosphere.

Enjoy a comfortable overnight stay at the hotel, with all meals included (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner).

Tree/1742544194347-332813104.jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Drive Sham Valley & Alchi -70km","day_summary":"

After a delightful breakfast, carry a packed lunch for the day's excursion. Our representative from Travelxploria, along with our experienced tour guide and best on-ground support staff, will accompany you on an enriching sightseeing journey.

Begin with a visit to the 1,000-year-old Alchi Monastery, the only Gompa in Ladakh situated on flat ground, offering a serene spiritual experience. Next, proceed to the Likir Monastery, home to the magnificent three-storey Dharma Wheel Gompa and an awe-inspiring massive Buddha statue.

Continue the journey to witness the breathtaking Indus & Zanskar River Sangam, followed by a scenic drive along the Indus River, where you will visit the sacred Pathar Sahib Gurdwara and the fascinating Magnetic Hill, where vehicles seemingly defy gravity.

The day’s adventure also includes a hiking experience at Basgo Palace, known for its historical ruins, before concluding with a visit to the Leh Hall of Fame, a museum dedicated to the Kargil War memorabilia, showcasing the valor of Indian soldiers.

Return to Leh for a comfortable overnight stay at your hotel, with all meals included (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner).

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Tour of Khardungla Pass (18380FT) & Stok","day_summary":"

After a hearty breakfast, embark on an exhilarating journey to Khardungla, the world’s highest motorable road at 18,380 ft above sea level. Accompanied by our representative from Travelxploria, along with our experienced tour guide and best on-ground support staff, you will witness the breathtaking snow-capped peaks and mesmerizing mountain views at Khardungla Pass.

In the afternoon, we proceed to Singhu Point, where the Indus River flows, offering a sacred site for holy baths. Next, explore the Stok Museum and Stok Palace, a 200-year-old historical marvel. The palace showcases an exquisite collection of royal tankhas, crowns, traditional dresses, ancient coins, precious stones, and turquoise headpieces, providing a glimpse into Ladakh’s regal past.

After an enriching day, return to your hotel for a delicious dinner and enjoy a comfortable overnight stay. (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner)

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Tour of Pangong Lake","day_summary":"

After an early breakfast, embark on a scenic journey to the magnificent Pangong Lake (155 km from Leh, approximately 5-hour drive each way) via the breathtaking Changla Pass (5,486 meters). Accompanied by our representative from Travelxploria, along with our experienced tour guide and best on-ground support staff, experience a seamless and enriching trip.

Nestled at an altitude of 14,000 feet (4,267 meters), Pangong Lake is a spectacular landlocked lake spanning over 130 km, with its waters shared between India and China. This stunning narrow basin of inland drainage stretches up to six to seven kilometers at its widest point. Once restricted, the lake is now open to tourists, although it remains under army surveillance.

Take in the unforgettable landscapes that surround this serene waterbody—offering a rare, intimate connection with nature. Spend some leisurely moments by the tranquil blue waters, capturing the breathtaking scenery in your memories.

After soaking in the pristine beauty of Pangong Lake, drive back to Leh for a delicious dinner and a comfortable overnight stay at the hotel. (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner)

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Depart Leh","day_summary":"

In the morning, our representative from Travelxploria, along with our experienced tour guide and best on-ground support staff, will ensure a smooth transfer to the airport. They will assist you with your luggage and all necessary formalities as you board your flight for your onward destination, carrying unforgettable memories of your incredible journey.

packages.overview:
1. Arrive Leh (3,505 m / 11,567 ft)
    Touch down at Leh airport and transfer to your hotel to rest and acclimatize.
    Spend the afternoon wandering the vibrant Leh bazaar and sampling local Tibetan‑style teas.

  2. Drive Sham Valley & Alchi (≈70 km)
    Cruise through the tranquil Sham Valley, stopping at Gurudwara Pathar Sahib and the Magnetic Hill illusion.
    Explore the 11th‑century Alchi Monastery’s exquisite frescoes and wooden carvings.

  3. Tour Khardung La → Nubra Valley & Hunder (120 km, 4–5 hrs)
    Depart early to conquer Khardung La (18,380 ft), the world’s highest motorable pass.
    Descend into Nubra Valley and wander among the dunes of Hunder’s Bactrian camel camps.

  4. Diskit Monastery (515 years old)
    After breakfast, visit Diskit Monastery to admire its towering Maitreya Buddha statue and ancient murals.
    Wander the peaceful courtyard and enjoy panoramic valley views from its hilltop perch.

  5. Tour Pangong Lake
    Head east via Chang La to reach the azure expanse of Pangong Lake by midday.
    Stroll the shoreline as its waters shimmer from deep blue to emerald green with the shifting light.

  6. Depart Leh
    Return to Leh for a farewell breakfast before transferring to the airport.
    Carry home memories of lofty passes, ancient monasteries, and shimmering Himalayan lakes.

Nubra Valley and Hundur Sand Dunes Drive 120 Kms 4/5hrs","day_summary":"

After a delicious breakfast, embark on an unforgettable journey to Nubra Valley via Khardung La Pass, the highest motorable road in the world at 18,380 ft. Our representative from Travelxploria, along with our experienced tour guide, will accompany you throughout the journey, ensuring a seamless and enriching experience.

Arrive at Hundur by afternoon, where our best on-ground support staff from Travelxploria will assist with check-in at your camp. After a refreshing break and lunch, the rest of the day is free to explore the enchanting Deskit and Hunder villages. Enjoy an exciting camel safari on the unique sand dunes between Deskit and Hunder, a breathtaking experience amidst the stunning landscapes of Nubra Valley.

Return to the camp for a relaxing evening, followed by dinner and an overnight stay, surrounded by the serene beauty of the valley.

After an early breakfast, our representative from Travelxploria, along with our experienced tour guide, will accompany you on an unforgettable journey to Pangong Lake, the highest landlocked lake in the world. The scenic drive, approximately 155 km from Leh (5 hours each way), takes you through the majestic Changla Pass (5,486m), offering spectacular mountain views.

Upon arrival at Pangong Lake, situated at an altitude of 14,000 feet (4,267m), immerse yourself in the breathtaking landscape. Spanning over 130 km, this stunning lake is bisected by the international border between India and China and remains under army surveillance. Take in the serenity of the crystal-clear blue waters, surrounded by mesmerizing mountains.

Our best on-ground support staff from Travelxploria ensures a seamless experience throughout the trip. After soaking in the beauty of Pangong Lake, we drive back to Leh, where you can relax and unwind at your hotel. Enjoy a delicious dinner and an overnight stay, reminiscing about this incredible adventure.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Depart Leh","day_summary":"

In the morning, our representative from Travelxploria, along with our experienced tour guide and best on-ground support staff, will ensure a smooth transfer to the airport for your onward journey. We bid you farewell with cherished memories of your incredible trip, hoping to welcome you again for another unforgettable adventure.

packages.overview:
1. Arrive Leh (3,505 m / 11,567 ft)
    Land at Leh airport and transfer to hotel for acclimatization.
    Relax and enjoy the scenic surroundings of the high-altitude city.

  2. Sham Valley & Alchi (70 km)
    Explore Magnetic Hill, Gurudwara Pathar Sahib, and Sangam Point.
    Visit the ancient Alchi Monastery, known for its beautiful frescoes.

  3. Leh → Nubra Valley via Khardung La (120 km, 4–5 hrs)
    Cross Khardung La, the world’s highest motorable pass at 18,380 ft.
    Visit Hunder to enjoy sand dunes and optional camel rides.

  4. Diskit Monastery Visit
    After breakfast, visit the 515-year-old Diskit Monastery.
    Witness the majestic Maitreya Buddha statue and panoramic valley views.

  5. Leh → Pangong Lake via Shey & Thiksey
    Drive through Chang La pass, visiting Shey Palace and Thiksey Monastery en route.
    Reach the stunning Pangong Lake, famous for its color-changing waters.

  6. Pangong Lake → Leh via Hemis Monastery
    Return to Leh via a scenic route, stopping at Hemis—the largest monastery in Ladakh.
    Enjoy your last evening in Leh with some local shopping or relaxation.

  7. Depart Leh
    Transfer to airport for your onward journey.
    Take back unforgettable memories of Ladakh's natural beauty and culture.

Arrive at Kushok Bakula Airport, Leh – 3500m above sea level. Your Travelxploria representative will be there to assist with the transfer to the hotel. Enjoy breakfast at the hotel before 09:30 am. Rest for half a day to acclimatize to the altitude.

Lunch will be served at the hotel. After lunch, embark on a drive to visit the Shanti Stupa and Leh Palace. Later, in the evening, take a leisurely walk around the local market.

Overnight stay at the hotel (Breakfast / Lunch / Dinner).

Thereafter proceed to the Likir Monastrey to visit the splendid three storeys Dharma Wheel Gompa. Here you’ll be awestruck with the sight of the massive Buddha statues., Indus & Zanskar river Sangam, drive along the Indus river visiting, Pathar Sahib Gurdwara and Magnetic Hill (where the cars defy gravity), Basgo Palace Hiking only and further on to Leh Hall of Fame (which has a museum of the Kargil War memorabilia) Overnight stay at the hotel (Breakfast /Lunch /Dinner)

After lunch, you will have the rest of the day free to explore Deskit and Hunder Villages. You can also enjoy a thrilling camel safari in the sand dunes between Deskit and Hunder Village.

Overnight stay at the camp (Breakfast / Lunch / Dinner).

packages.overview:
1. Arrive Leh (3,505 m / 11,567 ft)
    Arrive at Leh airport and transfer to your hotel for rest and acclimatization.
    Enjoy a relaxed evening soaking in the high-altitude atmosphere.

  2. Leh – Alchi – Lamayuru – Alchi/Ulleytokpo (180 km)
    Drive through dramatic landscapes to visit the moon-like terrain of Lamayuru and the ancient Alchi Monastery.
    Stay overnight in the peaceful village of Alchi or Ulleytokpo.

  3. Alchi/Ulleytokpo – Leh (70 km)
    Return to Leh with scenic stops along the way including Magnetic Hill and Sangam Point.
    Spend the evening at leisure in Leh’s colorful market or cafes.

  4. Leh → Nubra Valley via Khardung La (120 km, 4–5 hrs)
    Drive over Khardung La, the world’s highest motorable pass.
    Arrive in Nubra Valley and explore the Hunder sand dunes with optional camel rides.

  5. Visit Diskit Monastery
    After breakfast, visit the 515-year-old Diskit Monastery with its massive Maitreya Buddha statue.
    Enjoy breathtaking views of the valley from the monastery hilltop.

  6. Tour of Pangong Lake
    Travel via the scenic Shayok route to the beautiful Pangong Lake.
    Relax by the lake and admire its ever-changing shades of blue.

  7. Pangong → Leh via Hemis Monastery
    Return to Leh with a visit to Hemis Monastery, the largest and richest in Ladakh.
    Enjoy your last evening in Leh with some local shopping or rest.

  8. Depart Leh
    Transfer to Leh airport for your return journey.
    Depart with unforgettable memories of Ladakh’s landscapes and culture.

Upon arrival at Kushok Bakula Airport, Leh (3500m above sea level), our Travelxploria representative will assist with your transfer to the hotel. Enjoy a delicious breakfast at the hotel before 09:30 am. The rest of the morning will be free for rest and acclimatization.

After lunch at the hotel, you will be driven to visit Shanti Stupa and Leh Palace, with the chance to explore the local area. In the evening, take a leisurely stroll around the local market.

Overnight stay at the hotel.

Thereafter, our Travelxploria representative and experienced tour guide will lead you to the Likir Monastery, where you’ll marvel at the splendid three-story Dharma Wheel Gompa and the massive Buddha statues. Continue your journey to the Indus & Zanskar River Sangam, and drive along the Indus River, stopping to visit the Pathar Sahib Gurdwara and Magnetic Hill (where cars defy gravity).

Next, enjoy a short hike to Basgo Palace before proceeding to the Leh Hall of Fame, which houses a museum dedicated to the Kargil War memorabilia.

Overnight stay at the hotel.

Overnight stay at the camp.

After an early breakfast, your Travelxploria representative and experienced tour guide will accompany you on a scenic drive to Pangong Lake (14,500 ft), passing through Changla Pass (17,350 ft), the third highest motorable road in the world. Upon arrival at Pangong Lake, which is the highest saltwater lake in the world, shared by India and China, you'll have time to admire the stunning beauty of the lake. Relax by the shores, marveling at the changing colors and the fascinating high-altitude setting of the lake.

Overnight stay at the camp.

packages.overview:
1. Arrive Leh (3,505 m / 11,567 ft)
    Arrive at Leh airport and check into your hotel for acclimatization.
    Enjoy a relaxed evening soaking in the scenic beauty of the mountains.

  2. Sham Valley & Alchi (70 km)
    Explore Magnetic Hill, Sangam Point, and Gurudwara Pathar Sahib.
    Visit the 11th-century Alchi Monastery with its intricate murals.

  3. Leh → Nubra Valley via Khardung La (120 km / 4–5 hrs)
    Cross Khardung La, one of the highest motorable passes in the world.
    Explore Hunder’s sand dunes and enjoy a Bactrian camel ride.

  4. Visit Diskit Monastery
    Post breakfast, visit the 515-year-old Diskit Monastery.
    Admire the giant Maitreya Buddha and sweeping valley views.

  5. Tour of Pangong Lake
    Drive via the scenic Shayok route to reach the mesmerizing Pangong Lake.
    Relax by the lake’s changing hues and peaceful surroundings.

  6. Pangong → Leh
    Return to Leh with a scenic drive through Chang La Pass.
    Rest and unwind in Leh after a day of travel.

  7. Tour of Hemis, Thiksey & Shey + 3 Idiots School & Sindhu Darshan
    Visit iconic monasteries like Hemis, Thiksey, and Shey Palace.
    Explore Rancho's School (3 Idiots fame) and the Sindhu Darshan Ghat.

  8. Depart Leh
    Transfer to Leh airport for your onward journey.
    Leave with unforgettable memories of Ladakh’s beauty and heritage.

On arrival at Kushok Bakula Airport, Leh (3500m above sea level), our Travelxploria representative will assist you with your transfer to the hotel. Enjoy breakfast at the hotel before 09:30 am, followed by a half-day rest to acclimatize to the altitude.

After lunch at the hotel, our experienced Travelxploria tour guide will accompany you to visit Shanti Stupa and Leh Palace. Later in the evening, take a leisurely walk around the local market. Overnight stay at the hotel.

-70km","day_summary":"

After breakfast, our Travelxploria representative will ensure you have your packed lunch for the day. Accompanied by an experienced Travelxploria tour guide, embark on a fascinating sightseeing tour, starting with the 1000-year-old Alchi Monastery, the only Gompa in the Ladakh region on flat ground.

Next, proceed to the Likir Monastery to marvel at the splendid three-storey Dharma Wheel Gompa and the awe-inspiring massive Buddha statues.

Continue your journey to the confluence of the Indus and Zanskar rivers at the Sangam. Drive along the Indus River, visiting the Pathar Sahib Gurdwara and the famous Magnetic Hill, where cars appear to defy gravity.

Explore the Basgo Palace on a short hike, and end the day with a visit to Leh Hall of Fame, which houses a museum with Kargil War memorabilia.

Overnight stay at the hotel (Breakfast / Lunch / Dinner).

After an early breakfast, your Travelxploria representative, along with the experienced tour guide, will accompany you on your journey to Pangong Lake (14,500 ft) via the Changla Pass, which stands at 17,350 ft, making it the third-highest motorable road in the world.

Upon arrival at Pangong Lake, the highest saltwater lake in the world, shared by India and China, you can marvel at the stunning beauty of the lake. As you enjoy the breathtaking views from the banks of the lake, appreciate the changing colors and the fascinating high-altitude environment.

Overnight stay at the camp.

Idiots School Campus Sindhu Darshan","day_summary":"

After a leisurely breakfast, your Travelxploria representative and experienced tour guide will accompany you on a drive to visit Hemis Monastery, located 45 km west of Leh. Hemis is the largest and wealthiest monastery in Ladakh, and your guide will provide insightful details about its history and significance.

From Hemis, you'll be driven back to visit Thiksey Monastery, which is perched on a hill with breathtaking views of the Indus Valley. Thiksey is especially famous for its gigantic seated statue of Maitreya and its Dukhang assembly hall, home to hundreds of prayer books bound in silk.

Next, continue your journey through a series of chortens to Shey Palace, the former summer palace of the King of Ladakh. Then, visit the 3 Idiots School campus and the iconic Sindhu Darshan.

Overnight stay at the hotel (Breakfast / Lunch / Dinner).

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Depart Leh","day_summary":"

In the morning, your Travelxploria representative will assist you with the transfer to the airport to board your flight for your onward destination.

packages.overview:
1. Arrive Srinagar Airport – Begin your journey amidst the lush valleys of Kashmir.

  2. Srinagar – Sonamarg – Kargil (250 km / 7–8 hrs) – Scenic drive via Zoji La to reach Kargil.

  3. Kargil – Leh (6–7 hrs) – Drive through Lamayuru and Fotu La to reach Leh.

  4. Leh – Hemis – Thiksey – Shey – 3 Idiots School – Sindhu Darshan – Explore monasteries, heritage sites, and cultural landmarks.

  5. Leh – Khardung La – Nubra Valley – Hunder Sand Dunes (120 km / 4–5 hrs) – Cross the world’s highest motorable road into desert terrain.

  6. Visit Diskit Monastery – Explore the 515-year-old monastery and giant Maitreya Buddha.

  7. Nubra – Pangong Lake (155 km) – Drive via Shayok to the mesmerizing high-altitude lake.

  8. Pangong – Leh + Leh Palace & Shanti Stupa – Return via Chang La and enjoy panoramic views in Leh.

  9. Depart Leh – Transfer to airport for your onward journey.

Sonamarg Kargil 7/8 Hrs drive 250 Kms","day_summary":"

Depart from Srinagar as per road opening timings, accompanied by our Travelxploria representative and experienced tour guide. The drive to Sonamarg unveils the spectacular countryside of Kashmir in the Sindh Valley. The Sindhu Valley, the largest tributary of the Kashmir Valley, offers breathtaking views. Continue the journey towards Sonamarg (2740 Mtrs), with a scenic drive through panoramic villages. From Sonamarg, the road becomes rougher, passing through the wet Zojila Pass (3527 Mtrs), known as the Gateway to Ladakh. Continue towards Drass, the second coldest inhabited place in the world. After another two-and-a-half-hour drive, we will arrive in Kargil (2710 Mtrs), a town that gained prominence after Ladakh opened to tourism in 1974.

Overnight stay at the hotel. (Breakfast / Lunch / Dinner)

to Leh 6/7 Hrs drive","day_summary":"

Depart from Kargil after an early breakfast, accompanied by our Travelxploria representative and experienced tour guide. En route, visit the Mulbekh Monastery and Lamayuru Monastery, famous for its Moonlandscape, passing through Namkila and Fatula Passes, the highest points on the Srinagar-Leh road. Continue the drive towards Leh, stopping at the 1000-year-old Alchi Monastery, the only Gompa in the Ladakh region situated on flat ground. Proceed to Likir Monastery, where you’ll be awestruck by the splendid three-storey Dharma Wheel Gompa and the massive Buddha statues.

Next, visit the Indus & Zanskar River Sangam, and continue driving along the Indus River to visit Pathar Sahib Gurdwara and Magnetic Hill, where the cars defy gravity. Further on, stop at the Leh Hall of Fame, which houses a museum dedicated to the Kargil War memorabilia.

Overnight stay at the hotel. (Breakfast / Lunch / Dinner)

of Pangong Lake- It is the highest land locked lake (155 Kms One way)","day_summary":"

After an early breakfast, your Travelxploria representative and experienced tour guide will accompany you as you leave for Pangong Lake (14,500 ft), passing through Changla Pass (17,350 ft), the third highest motorable road in the world. Upon arrival at Pangong Lake, the highest saltwater lake in the world, shared by both India and China, you will be mesmerized by the breathtaking beauty of the lake. Take in the changing colors and appreciate the fascinating high-altitude surroundings. Our best on-ground support staff will be there to assist you throughout the journey.

Overnight stay at the hotel (Breakfast / Lunch / Dinner).

packages.overview:
1. Arrive Leh (3505m / 11,567 ft) – Arrive and acclimatize in the high-altitude town of Leh.

  2. Tour Hemis, Thiksey, Shey, 3 Idiots School & Sindhu Darshan – Explore monasteries and iconic local attractions.

  3. Leh – Alchi – Lamayuru – Alchi/Ulleytokpo (180 km) – Scenic drive to ancient monasteries and moonland terrain.

  4. Alchi/Ulleytokpo – Leh (70 km) – Return to Leh via Magnetic Hill and Sangam Point.

  5. Leh – Khardung La – Nubra Valley – Hunder Sand Dunes (120 km / 4–5 hrs) – Cross Khardung La to explore sand dunes and camel rides.

  6. Visit Diskit Monastery – Visit the 515-year-old monastery and giant Buddha statue.

  7. Nubra – Pangong Lake (155 km) – Drive via Shayok to the spectacular blue Pangong Lake.

  8. Pangong – Leh, Evening Free for Market – Return to Leh via Chang La and enjoy local shopping.

  9. Depart Leh – Transfer to the airport for your journey back.

[{"city":"Leh","country":"India","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/India/Leh/1744975614134-AdobeStock_119628565.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Leh/1744975614246-AdobeStock_151500785.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Leh/1744975614351-AdobeStock_166870851.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Leh/1744975614463-AdobeStock_175167969.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Arrive Leh; 3505 meters / 11567 feet.","day_summary":"

Upon your arrival at Kushok Bakula Airport, Leh (3,500m above sea level), our Travelxploria representative will warmly welcome you and assist with a smooth transfer to your hotel. Enjoy a delicious breakfast before 9:30 AM, followed by a half-day rest for acclimatization, ensuring a comfortable adjustment to the high altitude.

After a relaxing lunch at the hotel, embark on a sightseeing tour led by our experienced Travelxploria tour guide. Visit the iconic Shanti Stupa, offering panoramic views of Leh, followed by a tour of the historic Leh Palace, a magnificent 17th-century royal residence.

Later in the evening, take a leisurely stroll through the bustling Leh market, where you can explore local handicrafts, Tibetan artifacts, and souvenirs. Our best on-ground support staff will be available to assist you throughout your journey.

Overnight stay at the hotel. (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner included)

Ladakh/1742544515329-Dining-Area-at-Grand-Dragon.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Leh/The Grand Dragon Ladakh/1742544515402-new-hd-3.jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Tour of Hemis, Thiksey Shey monastery 3 Idiots School Campus Sindhu Darshan","day_summary":"

After a leisurely breakfast, our Travelxploria representative along with our experienced Travelxploria tour guide will accompany you on a mesmerizing journey to Hemis Monastery, located 45 km west of Leh. As the largest and wealthiest monastery in Ladakh, Hemis is renowned for its ancient relics, vibrant festivals, and spiritual ambiance.

Following this, we drive back along the same route to visit Thiksey Monastery, an architectural marvel perched atop a hill with breathtaking views of the Indus Valley. Thiksey is particularly famous for its gigantic seated statue of Maitreya Buddha and its Dukhang (assembly hall), which houses hundreds of sacred prayer books bound in silk and stacked between wooden covers.

Continuing our journey, we pass through a series of chortens to reach Shey Palace, the former summer palace of the King of Ladakh. Explore the historic charm of this royal residence before proceeding to visit the famous 3 Idiots School Campus and the Sindhu Darshan site, where you can witness the serene beauty of the Indus River.

Throughout the day, our best on-ground support staff will ensure your comfort and seamless experience.

Overnight stay at the hotel. (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner included)

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Ulleytokpo","country":"India","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Leh – Alchi – Lamayuru – Alchi/Ulleytokpo (180kms)","day_summary":"

After a delightful breakfast, our Travelxploria representative, along with our experienced Travelxploria tour guide and best on-ground support staff, will accompany you on an enriching sightseeing journey.

Begin the day with a visit to the 1000-year-old Alchi Monastery, the only Gompa in Ladakh built on flat ground, renowned for its ancient murals and serene spiritual ambiance.

Next, proceed towards the breathtaking Moon Landscape, a region known for its surreal, crater-like formations that resemble the surface of the moon. Continue the journey to Lamayuru Monastery, one of the oldest and most picturesque monasteries in Ladakh, offering panoramic views of the rugged terrain.

After exploring Lamayuru, we drive back to Ulleytokpo, where you can unwind and experience the tranquil surroundings of this scenic region.

Our dedicated Travelxploria team ensures a comfortable and seamless journey throughout the day.

Overnight stay at the camp. (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner included)

After a hearty breakfast, our Travelxploria representative, along with our experienced Travelxploria tour guide and best on-ground support staff, will ensure a smooth and enriching journey as we depart from Uleytokpo.

Our first stop is the Likir Monastery, home to the magnificent three-storey Dharma Wheel Gompa and an awe-inspiring giant Buddha statue. The serene and spiritual ambiance of this monastery is sure to leave you mesmerized.

We then proceed to witness the spectacular confluence of the Indus & Zanskar Rivers, a breathtaking natural wonder where the two rivers meet, creating a stunning contrast in colors.

Continuing the drive along the Indus River, we visit the Pathar Sahib Gurdwara, a revered Sikh shrine known for its spiritual significance and peaceful aura. Next, we head to the mystical Magnetic Hill, where vehicles appear to defy gravity, offering a truly fascinating experience.

Our journey further takes us to Basgo Palace, where you can enjoy an adventurous hiking experience amidst its ancient ruins.

Finally, we visit the Leh Hall of Fame, a museum dedicated to the brave soldiers of the Kargil War, where you can explore war memorabilia, historical exhibits, and patriotic tributes.

After a day full of exploration, we return to Leh, where our Travelxploria team ensures your seamless check-in at the hotel.

Overnight stay at the hotel. (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner included)

Nubra Valley and Hundur Sand Dunes – Drive 120 Kms 4/5hrs","day_summary":"

After a delightful breakfast, our Travelxploria representative, accompanied by our experienced Travelxploria tour guide and best on-ground support staff, will ensure a smooth and memorable journey as we embark on an adventurous drive to Nubra Valley via Khardungla Pass—the highest motorable road in the world at 18,380 ft.

Upon arrival at Hundur in the afternoon, our team will assist with your check-in at the camp. After a hearty lunch, you will have the rest of the day to explore the charming villages of Deskit and Hunder, where you can witness the rich cultural heritage of Ladakh.

One of the key highlights of this day is the camel safari in the mesmerizing sand dunes between Deskit and Hunder, where you will get a chance to ride the unique double-humped Bactrian camels amidst the stunning desert landscape.

After an eventful day, our Travelxploria team will ensure you have a comfortable and relaxing stay at the camp.

Overnight stay at the camp. (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner included)

After a delicious breakfast, our Travelxploria representative, along with our experienced Travelxploria tour guide and best on-ground support staff, will accompany you on a visit to the Deskit Monastery, the largest and oldest monastery in Nubra Valley. Here, you will be captivated by the majestic Maitreya Buddha statue and the breathtaking panoramic views of the valley.

Following the monastery visit, we embark on a scenic drive back to Leh via the iconic Khardungla Pass, the highest motorable road in the world (18,380 ft), where you can pause to capture stunning photographs of the snow-capped peaks and vast landscapes.

Upon arrival in Leh by the afternoon, our Travelxploria team will assist with check-in at your hotel. The evening is free for you to leisurely explore the vibrant Leh market, where you can shop for traditional handicrafts, souvenirs, and Tibetan artifacts.

Overnight stay at the hotel. (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner included)

of Pangong Lake- It is the highest land locked lake (155 Kms One way)","day_summary":"

After an early breakfast, our Travelxploria representative, accompanied by our experienced Travelxploria tour guide and best on-ground support staff, will escort you on an unforgettable journey to the breathtaking Pangong Lake (14,500 ft).

En route, traverse the majestic Changla Pass (17,350 ft), the third highest motorable road in the world, offering spectacular mountain views and a thrilling driving experience.

Upon arrival at Pangong Lake, marvel at the pristine blue waters of the highest saltwater lake in the world, which stretches across India and China. Witness the mesmerizing color-changing phenomenon of the lake, transitioning from shades of blue to green and even golden under the shifting sunlight.

Spend time on the serene lake banks, soaking in the peaceful atmosphere and the breathtaking high-altitude scenery.

Overnight stay at the camp. (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner included)

packages.overview:
1. Arrive Manali – Reach the beautiful hill town and prepare for your Himalayan journey.

  2. Manali – Sarchu (222 km / 4000m) – Drive over scenic high mountain passes to the campsite at Sarchu.

  3. Sarchu – Leh (220 km) – Journey through rugged terrain crossing Nakee La, Lachung La, and Tanglang La.

  4. Drive Sham Valley & Alchi (70 km) – Explore Magnetic Hill, Sangam Point, and the ancient Alchi Monastery.

  5. Leh – Khardung La – Nubra Valley – Hunder Sand Dunes (120 km / 4–5 hrs) – Cross Khardung La and experience desert scenery and double-humped camels.

  6. Visit Diskit Monastery – Visit the 515-year-old hilltop monastery with stunning valley views.

  7. Nubra – Pangong Lake via Shey & Thiksey (155 km) – Drive through Shayok Valley with visits to iconic monasteries en route to the magical Pangong Lake.

  8. Pangong – Leh via Hemis Monastery (170 km) – Return to Leh, stopping at Hemis—the richest monastery in Ladakh.

  9. Depart Leh – Transfer to the airport for your onward flight with cherished memories.

Early in the morning, our Travelxploria representative will assist you as you embark on a thrilling journey to Sarchu. Travel through the breathtaking Rohtang Pass (3,955 meters), soaking in stunning views of the Lahaul Valley, passing through Tandi, Keylong, and Jispa.

En route, take a refreshing lunch break at Darcha, where our experienced Travelxploria tour guide will share insights about the region’s unique terrain and culture.

After lunch, continue your drive through the majestic Baralacha Pass (4,845 meters), witnessing its rugged beauty before reaching Sarchu, where our best on-ground support staff will ensure a seamless check-in at your campsite.

Overnight stay at the camp.

After a hearty breakfast, our Travelxploria representative will assist you as you embark on an exhilarating drive to Leh, crossing some of the highest motorable passes in the world.

With our experienced Travelxploria tour guide providing fascinating insights, travel through Tanglang La (5,350 meters), the second-highest motorable road in the world, and Lachung La (5,065 meters), both offering breathtaking panoramic views of the rugged Himalayan terrain.

Upon arrival in Leh, our best on-ground support staff will ensure a seamless check-in at your hotel, where you can relax and unwind after a thrilling day.

Overnight stay at the hotel. (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner included)

After a delightful breakfast, our Travelxploria representative will provide you with a packed lunch for the day as you embark on an enriching sightseeing tour.

Accompanied by our experienced Travelxploria tour guide, begin your journey with a visit to the 1,000-year-old Alchi Monastery, the only Gompa in Ladakh situated on flat ground, offering a glimpse into ancient Buddhist art and culture. Next, proceed to the Likir Monastery, where you’ll be awestruck by the splendid three-story Dharma Wheel Gompa and the massive Buddha statues dominating the landscape.

Continue to the spectacular Indus & Zanskar river Sangam, where the two rivers merge in a striking contrast of colors. Drive along the Indus River, visiting the revered Pathar Sahib Gurdwara and witnessing the mysterious phenomenon at Magnetic Hill, where cars appear to defy gravity.

Explore Basgo Palace, accessible via a short hike, and later, visit the Leh Hall of Fame, a museum dedicated to the bravery of Indian soldiers with memorabilia from the Kargil War.

Our best on-ground support staff will ensure a seamless experience throughout the day.

Overnight stay at the hotel. (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner included)

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Nubra Valley","country":"India","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/India/Nubra Valley/1744976893350-AdobeStock_168464034.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Nubra Valley/1744976893938-AdobeStock_362520422.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Tour of Khardungla Pass 18380Ft. Nubra Valley and Hundur Sand Dunes – Drive 120 Kms 4/5hrs","day_summary":"

After a hearty breakfast, our Travelxploria representative will assist you as you embark on an exciting journey to Nubra Valley, traversing the legendary Khardung La Pass (18,380 ft)—the highest motorable road in the world. Enjoy breathtaking views of the snow-clad mountains as our experienced Travelxploria tour guide shares fascinating insights about the region’s history and culture.

Arrive at Hundur by afternoon, where our best on-ground support staff will ensure a smooth check-in at your camp. After a delicious lunch, you’ll have the rest of the day free to explore Deskit and Hunder villages, immersing yourself in the unique charm of Ladakh.

In the evening, experience an unforgettable camel safari in the enchanting sand dunes between Deskit and Hunder Village, where the double-humped Bactrian camels add to the magical desert landscape.

Overnight stay at the camp. (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner included)

After a delicious breakfast, our Travelxploria representative will accompany you on a visit to the Deskit Monastery, the oldest and largest monastery in Nubra Valley, offering breathtaking views of the surrounding mountains and the iconic Maitreya Buddha statue.

Following the monastery visit, begin your journey back to Leh, retracing the scenic route via the mighty Khardung La Pass (18,380 ft). Our experienced Travelxploria tour guide will enrich the journey with insights into the strategic and cultural significance of this high-altitude marvel.

Arrive in Leh by afternoon, where our best on-ground support staff will assist with check-in at your hotel. The evening is free for you to leisurely explore the vibrant Leh market, where you can shop for local handicrafts, souvenirs, and traditional Ladakhi artifacts.

Overnight stay at the hotel. (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner included)

After an early breakfast, our Travelxploria representative will assist you as you embark on a mesmerizing journey to Pangong Lake (14,500 ft). Accompanied by our experienced Travelxploria tour guide, travel through the breathtaking Changla Pass (17,350 ft)—the third highest motorable road in the world, offering spectacular mountain views.

Upon arrival at Pangong Lake, be captivated by its pristine blue waters and the surreal beauty of this high-altitude saltwater lake, which is uniquely shared by India and China. Marvel at the ever-changing colors of the lake, ranging from deep blue to turquoise, depending on the sunlight and the time of day.

Our best on-ground support staff will ensure a seamless check-in at your camp near the lake, allowing you to relax and soak in the tranquility of this magical destination.

Overnight stay at the camp. (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner included)

En route, visit the famous Hemis Monastery, the largest and wealthiest monastery in Ladakh, where our experienced Travelxploria tour guide will provide insights into its rich history, Buddhist artifacts, and vibrant festivals.

Upon arrival in Leh, our best on-ground support staff will assist with check-in at your hotel. The evening is free for you to explore the Leh market, where you can shop for local handicrafts, Tibetan jewelry, Pashmina shawls, and souvenirs.

packages.overview:
1. Arrive Leh (3505 m / 11,567 ft) – Arrive and acclimatize in the beautiful mountain town of Leh.

  2. Drive to Sham Valley & Alchi (70 km) – Explore Magnetic Hill, Sangam, and the ancient Alchi Monastery.

  3. Leh – Khardung La – Nubra Valley – Hunder (120 km / 4–5 hrs) – Cross the world’s highest motorable pass to reach Nubra’s sand dunes.

  4. Visit Diskit Monastery – Explore the historic 515-year-old monastery with stunning views.

  5. Nubra – Pangong Lake via Shey & Thiksey (155 km) – Scenic drive to Pangong with visits to Shey Palace and Thiksey Monastery.

  6. Pangong – Leh via Hemis Monastery – Return to Leh, stopping at the famous Hemis Monastery en route.

  7. Leh – Chumathang – Tsomoriri (240 km / 6 hrs) – Drive to the pristine Tsomoriri Lake through hot springs and remote landscapes.

  8. Tsomoriri – Tsokar (105 km / 3 hrs) – Travel to Tsokar, a lesser-known salt lake surrounded by wildlife.

  9. Tsokar – Taglang La – Upshi – Leh (150 km / 5–6 hrs) – Cross Taglang La Pass and return to Leh via scenic routes.

  10. Depart Leh – Transfer to the airport with unforgettable memories of Ladakh.

[{"city":"Leh","country":"India","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/India/Leh/1744975614134-AdobeStock_119628565.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Leh/1744975614246-AdobeStock_151500785.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Leh/1744975614351-AdobeStock_166870851.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Leh/1744975614463-AdobeStock_175167969.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Arrive Leh; 3505 meters / 11567 feet.","day_summary":"

Arrive at Kushok Bakula Rimpochee Airport, Leh (3,500m above sea level), where our Travelxploria representative will warmly welcome you and assist with a smooth transfer to your hotel. Enjoy a refreshing breakfast at the hotel before 9:30 AM, followed by a half-day rest for acclimatization, ensuring a comfortable adjustment to the high altitude.

Savor a delicious lunch at the hotel, and in the afternoon, embark on a guided sightseeing tour with our experienced Travelxploria tour guide. Visit the serene Shanti Stupa, offering breathtaking panoramic views of Leh, and explore the historic Leh Palace, a magnificent architectural marvel.

Later in the evening, take a leisurely walk around the local market, soaking in the vibrant culture and shopping for souvenirs. Throughout the day, our best on-ground support staff will be available to assist you, ensuring a seamless and enjoyable experience.

Overnight stay at the hotel.

Tree/1742544194347-332813104.jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Drive Sham Valley & Alchi -70km","day_summary":"

After a delightful breakfast, our Travelxploria representative will provide you with a packed lunch for the day’s adventure. Accompanied by our experienced Travelxploria tour guide, begin your sightseeing tour with a visit to the 1,000-year-old Alchi Monastery, the only Gompa in the Ladakh region situated on flat ground.

Next, proceed to the Likir Monastery, where you’ll be captivated by the magnificent three-storey Dharma Wheel Gompa and the awe-inspiring giant Buddha statues. Continue the journey to witness the breathtaking confluence of the Indus and Zanskar Rivers, a sight that will leave you spellbound.

Drive along the scenic Indus River, stopping to visit the sacred Pathar Sahib Gurdwara and experience the fascinating Magnetic Hill, where vehicles appear to defy gravity. The adventure continues with a hike to Basgo Palace, offering panoramic views of the Ladakhi landscape. Finally, visit the Leh Hall of Fame, a museum dedicated to the bravery of Indian soldiers, showcasing Kargil War memorabilia.

Throughout the day, our best on-ground support staff will be available to assist you, ensuring a seamless and enriching experience.

Overnight stay at the hotel.

After a delightful breakfast, our Travelxploria representative will assist you as you embark on an exhilarating drive to Nubra Valley via Khardung La (18,380 ft), the highest motorable road in the world. Enjoy breathtaking views along the way as our experienced Travelxploria tour guide shares fascinating insights about the region’s history and geography.

Arrive at Hundar by afternoon, where our best on-ground support staff will ensure a smooth check-in at your camp. After a refreshing lunch, spend the rest of the day exploring Deskit and Hundar villages, soaking in the serene beauty of the valley. Don’t miss the thrilling camel safari on the sand dunes between Deskit and Hundar, an experience unique to this region.

Overnight stay at the camp.

After a delightful breakfast, our Travelxploria representative will assist you as you set out on a scenic journey to Tsomoriri Lake. Enjoy a comfortable drive along the Indus River, passing through charming Ladakhi villages and soaking in the breathtaking landscapes. With insights from our experienced Travelxploria tour guide, learn about the region’s unique culture and geography.

Upon reaching Mahey Bridge, cross the Indus River and enter a stunning narrow gorge, leading to the pristine beauty of Tsomoriri Lake. The picturesque drive offers mesmerizing views of the rugged terrain and high-altitude landscapes.

Arrive at Korzok Village, nestled next to the lake, where our best on-ground support staff will ensure a smooth check-in at your camp. In the evening, take a leisurely walk around Tsomoriri Lake, admiring its tranquil beauty and ever-changing colors.

Overnight stay at the camp.

Early in the morning, our Travelxploria representative will accompany you to Tsomoriri Lake to witness a breathtaking sunrise, as the golden hues reflect over the tranquil waters, creating a mesmerizing sight.

After breakfast, embark on a scenic drive to Tsokar Lake, with our experienced Travelxploria tour guide providing fascinating insights into the region’s landscapes and wildlife. Retracing the route for about 50 kilometers, reach Sumdah, a small Tibetan settlement, before turning left towards Puga Valley, a paradise for birdwatchers where you may spot a variety of unique bird species.

From Puga Valley, continue the journey by crossing a small mountain pass before arriving at Tsokar Lake, a picturesque high-altitude salt lake. Learn about the nomadic communities who once traded in rock salt extracted from Tsokar.

Our best on-ground support staff will ensure a comfortable check-in at your camp, providing you with all necessary assistance for a relaxing stay.

Overnight stay at the camp.

After a serene visit to Tsokar Lake in the morning, our Travelxploria representative will assist you with your journey back. Enjoy a hearty breakfast at the camp before setting off on an adventurous drive westward towards the Leh-Manali Highway.

With our experienced Travelxploria tour guide sharing fascinating insights along the way, turn left at Mor-E Plains and begin the thrilling ascent to Taglang La Pass, one of the highest motorable passes in the world. From here, descend towards Upshi, taking in the breathtaking landscapes before continuing your drive back to Leh.

Throughout the journey, our best on-ground support staff will ensure a smooth and comfortable experience, making sure every aspect of your trip is well taken care of.

Arrive in Leh and check in at your hotel.

packages.overview:
1. Arrive Manali – Begin your trans-Himalayan adventure in the charming hill town of Manali.

  2. Manali – Sarchu (222 km / 4000 m) – Drive through the high passes of the Himalayas to reach Sarchu.

  3. Sarchu – Tsomoriri (220 km / 6 hrs) – Travel to the serene high-altitude Tsomoriri Lake, home to migratory birds.

  4. Tsomoriri – Leh (240 km / 7–8 hrs) – Scenic journey through remote landscapes and mountain passes to Leh.

  5. Drive to Sham Valley & Alchi (70 km) – Visit Sangam Point, Magnetic Hill, and the historic Alchi Monastery.

  6. Leh – Khardung La – Nubra Valley – Hunder (120 km / 4–5 hrs) – Cross the world’s highest motorable road into Nubra’s desert-like expanse.

  7. Visit Diskit Monastery – Explore Ladakh’s oldest monastery and its giant Maitreya Buddha statue.

  8. Nubra – Pangong Lake via Shey & Thiksey (155 km) – Drive through Shayok Valley and visit iconic monasteries en route to Pangong.

  9. Pangong – Leh via Hemis Monastery (170 km) – Return to Leh with a visit to the famous Hemis Monastery on the way.

  10. Depart Leh – Transfer to the airport with unforgettable memories of Ladakh’s majestic landscapes.

Early in the morning, our Travelxploria representative will assist you as you embark on a scenic journey to Sarchu. Accompanied by our experienced Travelxploria tour guide, travel through the breathtaking Rohtang Pass (3,955 meters), Lahaul Valley, Tandi, Keylong, and Jispa, with a refreshing lunch break at Darcha.

After lunch, continue the drive to Sarchu, passing through the majestic Baralacha Pass (4,845 meters). Throughout the journey, our best on-ground support staff will ensure your comfort and seamless travel experience.

Overnight stay at the camp in Sarchu.

After a delightful breakfast, our Travelxploria representative will assist you as you set off on a scenic drive to Pang. En route, with guidance from our experienced Travelxploria tour guide, you may spot wild animals roaming freely in their natural habitat. Pang is a tiny, seasonal market along the Leh-Manali route, operated by Ladakhi traders who migrate here from mid-June to September.

Crossing Pang, the journey continues uphill before reaching an expansive 60 km-long plain stretch, leading to the base of Tanglang La Pass (5,400 meters). Upon arrival at Tsomoriri, our team will facilitate a smooth check-in at your fixed camp.

Later, visit the picturesque lakefront and the small settlement of huts, soaking in the breathtaking beauty of Tsomoriri Lake, located 4,573 meters above sea level in southeast Leh. With its pearl-like shape and rich mineral deposits, the lake spans a 36 km circumference. Here, our best on-ground support staff will assist you in registering and obtaining the necessary permits.

Overnight stay at the camp.

After a delicious breakfast, our Travelxploria representative will provide you with a packed lunch for the day’s adventure. Accompanied by our experienced Travelxploria tour guide, begin your sightseeing tour with a visit to the 1,000-year-old Alchi Monastery, the only Gompa in the Ladakh region situated on flat ground.

Next, proceed to the Likir Monastery, where you’ll be captivated by the magnificent three-storey Dharma Wheel Gompa and the awe-inspiring giant Buddha statues. Continue the journey to witness the breathtaking confluence of the Indus and Zanskar Rivers, a sight that will leave you spellbound.

Drive along the scenic Indus River, stopping to visit the sacred Pathar Sahib Gurdwara and experience the fascinating Magnetic Hill, where vehicles appear to defy gravity. The adventure continues with a hike to Basgo Palace, offering panoramic views of the Ladakhi landscape. Finally, visit the Leh Hall of Fame, a museum dedicated to the bravery of Indian soldiers, showcasing Kargil War memorabilia.

Throughout the day, our best on-ground support staff will be available to assist you, ensuring a seamless and enriching experience.

Overnight stay at the hotel.

Nubra Valley and Hundur Sand Dunes – Drive 120 Kms 4/5hrs","day_summary":"

After a delightful breakfast, our Travelxploria representative will assist you as you embark on an exhilarating drive to Nubra Valley via Khardung La (18,380 ft), the highest motorable road in the world. Enjoy breathtaking views along the way as our experienced Travelxploria tour guide shares fascinating insights about the region’s history and geography.

Arrive at Hundar by afternoon, where our best on-ground support staff will ensure a smooth check-in at your camp. After a refreshing lunch, spend the rest of the day exploring Deskit and Hundar villages, soaking in the serene beauty of the valley. Don’t miss the thrilling camel safari on the sand dunes between Deskit and Hundar, an experience unique to this region.

Overnight stay at the camp.

packages.package_name: Echoes of Paradise: Kashmir to Leh Expedition
packages.overview:
• Dal Lake Shikara Ride – Glide over shimmering waters, surrounded by floating gardens and Srinagar’s breathtaking, tranquil landscapes.
  • Mughal Gardens & Shankaracharya Temple – Wander through lush, symmetrical gardens before admiring panoramic views from a sacred hilltop temple.
  • Gulmarg Gondola Ride – Ascend through crisp mountain air to snow-laden peaks, soaking in spectacular, alpine scenery.
  • Sonmarg & Thajiwas Glacier – Trek through pristine, icy wonderlands framed by towering, majestic mountains.
  • Srinagar to Leh via Kargil – Journey through mesmerizing, rugged terrain, capturing Ladakh’s historic and breathtaking mountain passes.
  • Nubra Valley & Hunder Dunes – Ride double-humped camels across golden, windswept sand dunes amid striking, dramatic landscapes.
  • Pangong Lake Escape – Marvel at crystal-clear, ever-changing blue waters reflecting Ladakh’s surreal, high-altitude serenity.
  • Magnetic Hill & Gurudwara Pathar Sahib – Witness mind-bending optical illusions before finding spiritual tranquility in a sacred, peaceful haven.
  • Hemis & Alchi Monasteries – Immerse in Ladakh’s rich, monastic heritage, admiring ancient frescoes and sacred relics.
  • Leh Market Stroll – Discover vibrant, handcrafted treasures, capturing the essence of Ladakh’s artistic and cultural charm.
